tagline: Stone-ground from traditionally sprouted organic wheat, this atta retains the full bran and germ — delivering richer flavour, higher nutrition, and superior digestibility compared to regular whole wheat flour.
why_love_it_1: Sprouting activates natural enzymes that pre-digest starches, making each roti lighter and easier to digest
why_love_it_2: Higher iron, zinc, and B-vitamins than regular atta due to the sprouting process
why_love_it_3: Stone-milling at low temperature preserves heat-sensitive nutrients and natural flavour
why_love_it_4: Sourced from verified organic farms in Madhya Pradesh — zero pesticides, zero chemicals
why_love_it_5: Ideal for rotis, parathas, and baked goods — your family's everyday flour, elevated
ingredients: Organic Sprouted Whole Wheat (100%). Manufactured in a facility that also processes gluten-containing grains.
how_to_use: Use like regular atta. For rotis: mix with warm water to a soft dough, rest for 15 minutes, then roll and cook on a hot tawa. Can be blended with other Dhatu flours for variety.

product_name: Dhatu Organics Wild Forest Honey — Raw & Unfiltered
tagline: Harvested by tribal communities from untouched forest hives in the Western Ghats, this raw honey is never heated, never filtered — just pure wildflower nectar with all its enzymes, pollens, and antioxidants intact.
bullet_3: Ethically sourced from tribal forest communities
bullet_4: No added sugar · No heating · No filtering
why_love_it_1: Raw honey retains live enzymes (diastase, invertase) that are destroyed by commercial heating
why_love_it_2: Each batch carries the unique floral signature of the season's bloom — naturally varied in colour and taste
why_love_it_3: Supports tribal livelihoods — sourced through fair-trade partnerships in the Western Ghats
why_love_it_4: Natural antimicrobial properties make it a traditional remedy for sore throats and minor wounds
why_love_it_5: May crystallise naturally over time — a sign of purity. Gently warm the jar in water to re-liquefy
ingredients: 100% Raw Wild Forest Honey. May contain traces of pollen and beeswax. Not suitable for children under 1 year.
how_to_use: Take 1 teaspoon directly or mix into warm (not hot) water, herbal tea, or milk. Use as a natural sweetener in smoothies and dressings. Store at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

tagline: A quick-cook breakfast grain made from certified organic little millet (samai), traditionally valued in South India for its light, cooling properties. Ready in 5 minutes — wholesome, gluten-free, and packed with minerals.
bullet_4: Low glycaemic index · Ideal for all ages
why_love_it_1: Little millet is one of India's oldest cultivated grains — naturally drought-resistant and grown without irrigation
why_love_it_2: Higher calcium content than most cereals — supports bone health for children and adults
why_love_it_3: The light, cooling nature of samai makes it excellent for summer porridges and weaning foods
why_love_it_4: Certified gluten-free — safe for those with celiac disease or gluten sensitivity
why_love_it_5: Sourced from organic dryland farms in Karnataka — supporting traditional millet cultivation
ingredients: Organic Little Millet (100%). Gluten-free. Processed in a dedicated gluten-free facility.
how_to_use: Porridge: bring 200ml water or milk to a boil, add 4 tbsp millet flakes, stir and cook 3–5 minutes on low heat. Sweeten with jaggery or honey. Alternatively, soak overnight and use uncooked in smoothie bowls.
